title: Recommend & Watch - Knowledge Check
description: Confirm you understand dtwiz recommendations, watch and setup.

questions.title: What does `dtwiz recommend` return?
questions.content: What is the output of `dtwiz recommend`?

options.text: A ranked list of Dynatrace ingestion methods for this system, with the reasoning behind each
options.explanation: It combines the local analysis with tenant state and ranks what to install next.

options.text: A list of Dynatrace problems detected in the tenant
options.explanation: Problem feeds live in the tenant UI/API - recommend is about instrumentation choices.

options.text: Sizing recommendations for your Kubernetes cluster
options.explanation: recommend targets observability coverage, not capacity planning.

questions.explanation: recommend = ranked ingestion methods (OneAgent, Operator, OTel, cloud) tailored to what analyze found.
hints: It even marks what is already covered (like a running OneAgent).

questions.title: Why did `dtwiz watch` need a different token?
questions.content: In this lab `dtwiz watch` refused to run with the injected access token. Why?

options.text: watch queries the platform APIs, which require a platform token (dt0s16) via DT_PLATFORM_TOKEN
options.explanation: Classic dt0c01 tokens cover the classic APIs (status, recommend, installs) but not the platform data queries watch uses.
GitHub is only involved when downloading dtwiz itself.
The same token kept working for recommend - it is a token TYPE difference, not expiry.

questions.explanation: watch live-tails newly ingested data through platform APIs - that requires DT_PLATFORM_TOKEN (dt0s16).
hints: Remember the token-type info box in Section 02.

questions.title: What does `dtwiz setup` chain together?
questions.content: You used the individual commands in this lab. What does the interactive `dtwiz setup` do?

options.text: analyze → recommend → install, as one guided interactive flow
options.explanation: setup is the one-command wizard combining everything you did by hand.

options.text: It only configures dtwiz's own config file
options.explanation: setup drives the full analyze/recommend/install flow, not just configuration.
That would be `dtwiz uninstall self` plus the installer.

questions.explanation: setup = the interactive wizard: analyze the system, recommend methods, install the chosen one.
hints: Its help text says: analyze, recommend, and install the best ingestion method.
